test.name: Resolve birthplace conflict via informant proximity and temporal distance
test.description: Three sources disagree on birthplace. Two contemporary census records (1850, 1860) say Ireland, one later death certificate (1908) says Pennsylvania. Tests whether the skill weighs informant proximity and temporal distance correctly per the GPS preponderance hierarchy.

input.user_message: Check for conflicts in Patrick Flynn's evidence and resolve any you find.

additional_criteria: Should note that the two census informants may be the same household member (likely Thomas Flynn or wife), which weakens their independence for this specific fact even though the sources themselves are independent

Resolution should cite both informant proximity (household_member vs family_not_present) and temporal distance (contemporary 1850/1860 recordings vs 63-year-later 1908 recollection) as factors favoring the census assertions
Should identify the son-in-law James Brown as a secondary informant for birth facts on the death certificate — he was not present at Patrick's birth and is reporting what he was told
Should set preferred_assertion_id to a_002 (or a_009 — both say Ireland) and status to resolved
